sutta: the buddha said long ago monks there was a teacher named arako a core setter freed of all lustful passions and he had many hundreds of disciples now this was the doctrine that he taught his disciples short is the life of man o brahmin insign o brahman or insignificant trifling fraught with much ill with much trouble by mantras awaken the people do good live the holy life for the born there is no immortality just stop here for a moment here this uh of course at arako he is talking to his disciples so he called them brahman or brahmana in the olden days the penuncians were called brahmanas because uh they were uh initially uh the renuncians were of the uh what we know now called the brahmin sec but long ago they were called brahmanas and then this external ascetic araku is he's 1 of those who teach mantras mantras are different from sutas sutas that we chant they consist of words with meanings but mantras are words which have no meaning to us there is no meaning to us but there is uh there is something about these um mantras uh which uh make them quite powerful because they seem to invoke some uh spirits so this um this uh araco continued he was teaching his disciples he said this as a drop of dew on the on the tip of a blade of grass when the sun gets up straight away dries up and lasts not a while even so brahman like a dew drop is the life of man insignificant trifling fraud with much ill and trouble by mantras awaken the people do good live the holy life for the born there is no immortality just as a bubble appears on the water when the sky deva rains down big drops but straight away bursts and lasts not a while even so brahman like a water bubble is the life of man insignificant trifling fraud with much ill and trouble for the born there is no immortality just as the line of a stick on water straight away vanishes and lasts not a while even so brahman like the line of a stick on water is the life of man insignificant trifling fraud with ill and trouble for the born there is no immortality just as a mountain river winding here and there swiftly flowing taking all along with it never for a moment or for an instant or for a second pauses but rushes on swirls along and sweeps forward even so brahmana like a mountain river is the life of man insignificant trifling fraud with ill and trouble for the born there is no immortality just as a strong man might fashion a gob of spittle on the tip of his tongue and spit it out with utmost ease even saw a brahmana like a spittle gob is the life of man insignificant trifling fraud with ill and trouble for the born there is no immortality just as a lump of meat thrown into a hot into an iron pot heated all day straight away splits up and lasts not a while even so a brahmana like a lump of meat is the life of man insignificant trifling fraught with ill and trouble for the born there is no immortality just as a cow about to be slaughtered being led to the shambles each time she raises her foot is nearer to destruction nearer to death even so a brahmana like a doom cow is the life of man insignificant trifling fraud with much ill and much trouble by mantras awaken the people do good live the holy life for the born there is no immortality now at that time monks the span of man's life was 60 thousand years and at 5 100 years girls were ripe to win 10 men had but 6 afflictions namely cold and heat hunger and thirst and the 2 whole excrement yet though such was the longevity such a duration and such the freedom from affliction of the people the teacher arako taught this doctrine to his disciples shawn is the life of man o brahman insignificant and trifling fraud with much ill much trouble by mantras awaken the people do good live the holy life for the born there is no immortality now monks if a man would speak truly he should say sean is the life of man insignificant and trifling fraught with ill and trouble by wisdom awaken the people do good live the holy life for the born there is no immortality for today monks he who lives long lives but a 100 years or a little more and though a man lives a 100 years he lives by 300 seasons 100 seasons of winter 100 seasons of summer and 100 seasons of rain and though he lived 300 seasons he lives by 1200 months 400 months of winter 400 months of summer and 400 months of rain and though who live 1200 months he lives but 4 and 20 100 fortnights 8 100 fortnights of winter 8 100 4 nights of summer and 8 100 fortnights of rain and though he lived 4 and 20 100 fortnights he lives but 6 and 60 thousand days 6 and 30 thousand days 12 thousand days of winter 12 thousand days of summer and 12 thousand days of rain and though he lives 6 and 30 thousand days he eats but 2 and 70 thousand meals 4 and 20 thousand meals in winter 4 and 20 000 meals in summer and 420 000 meals in the rainy season this includes mother's milk and foodless times here by foodless times is men agitated he eats no food grieved he eats no food ill he eats no food fasting he eats no food and not getting any he eats no food thus thus monks i account the life of man who lives a 100 years his lifespan the seasons the years the months the fortnights the days the days and nights meal times and foodless times so the buddha continued the last part monks the work to be done by a teacher for his disciples seeking their good by compassion because of compassion that has been done for you by me monks at the at the foot of these trees in these empty places cultivate and jhana amongst be not slothful let there be no occasion for you to reproach yourselves afterwards this is our command to you as the end of the sutta
commentary: and it's quite an interesting suitable you can see here this course sector arakoa he can be considered an external sec teacher because he does not teach the buddha dhamma yet we can see that there is some good dharma in his teaching since he was telling people about the impermanence of life and the urgency to cultivate the spiritual path and the similes given in his teaching are quite good for example he says this has a drop of dew on the tip of a blade of grass when the sun gets up straight away dries up and lasts not a while even so like a dew drop is the life of man just as a bubble appears on the water when the sky diva rains down big drops but straight away bursts and lasts not a while even so like a water bubble is the life of men etc etc so you find his his teachings here are quite good now we find this utah is mentioned that during his days a man's life span was 60 000 years in the sutras we find that man's lifespan can go up to a maximum of 80 thousand years on the average here during arakos time it was 60 thousand years and a woman could give birth at 500 years and at that time there were only 6 types of sickness of afflictions at that time you have to suffer cold you have to suffer heat hunger thirst and you have to urinate and um empty your bowels these were the 6 afflictions and even then thought that life was short even a lifespan of 60 000 years he says is short so how much more how much shorter is life nowadays because when our average lifespan is only about 70 to 75 years so the there should be more urgency for us to cultivate to the spiritual path because very soon we will be passing away from this world you also notice that arako taught the people to recite the mantra that was supposed to be the buddha's previous life but but in the buddha when after he was enlightened in our theravada sutas the earliest buddha never taught the mantras the buddha thought the way to attain wisdom which is the the arden 8 world path uh so in our theravada there is no mantra teaching now now we said that life is shorter if we consider this way suppose a person is 40 years old and we know that we live up to about 70 years old that means we have only 30 more years to go which means that we have only 30 more new years to celebrate and 1 goes by we have 29 and another goes by you have 28. if we look at it that way and then we can see here that our time is very very limited so because of that now we should constantly think of impermanence in the sati patanas we are taught to contemplate on the different types of corpses and that is a very good contemplation how in a few more years time now our body will turn into the cops at different stages of rotting different stages of decay we constantly contemplate that then it helps us to cut the conceit i am the the feeling that i exist it helps us to cut them and then the last part of the sutra the buddha tells the monks not to be slothful but to cultivate jhana actually sometimes some sutas they translated as meditate monks but actually the word is jayati jayathi is to cultivate jhana because in the buddha's teachings in the theravada meditation is synonymous with cultivating ghana that's why we can see on the arden 8-4 path the last factor is jhana and also in 1 of the earliest uttars we heard the buddha said samadhi margot samadhi is the way no samadhi is the wrong way so from there when buddhism went over to china and they started the meditation school they call it the chan that means the zen school and the word chan comes from the word channel which is jhana so the meditation school is actually the jhana school in the china and the next 2 ties room
